title: podman exec fails with "process_linux.go:86: executing setns process caused \"exit status 21\"
body:
**Environment details**

> 
> $ cat /etc/redhat-release 
> Fedora release 28 (Twenty Eight)
> 
> $ uname -a
> Linux localhost.localdomain 4.17.19-200.fc28.x86_64 #1 SMP Fri Aug 24 15:47:41 UTC 2018 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
> 
> $ podman --version
> podman version 0.8.4

**Reproducer steps:**

1. Run the container as a non-root user on terminal one

```
$ podman run --name test-exec -it fedora:28 /bin/bash
[root@7891d995088c /]# 
```

2. Now open a new terminal

```
$ podman exec -it test-exec bash
exec failed: container_linux.go:336: starting container process caused "process_linux.go:86: executing setns process caused \"exit status 21\""
exit status 1
```

3. The same steps work correctly as root user.

What could be the issue? 

Thanks,
Anil

comments.body:
Works fine with `podman-0.9.1-3.gitaba58d1.fc28.x86_64`

```
$ podman run --name test-exec -it fedora:28 /bin/bash                                                                                                            
Trying to pull docker.io/fedora:28...Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:565884f490d9ec697e519c57d55d09e268542ef2c1340fd63262751fa308f047
 82.90 MB / 82.90 MB [======================================================] 3s
Copying config sha256:c582c1438f27b3775e2534abc82d14974ecb00c2c53161d05ec73a73d35e1235                                                                                      
 2.29 KB / 2.29 KB [========================================================] 0s
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
[root@7d9987a43938 /]# 
```

And in other terminal:
```
$ podman exec -it test-exec bash                                                                                                                                 
[root@7d9987a43938 /]# 
```

Yes. It works fine on podman-0.9.1-3.gitaba58d1.fc28.x86_64. 
No errors.
Thanks,
Tomas
